question: The large holes in Swiss cheese are due to production of a large amount of CO₂ by Propionibacterium shermanii.
Microbes are used in the production of fermented beverages like wine, beer, whisky, brandy, rum, etc.
Antibiotics are chemical substances produced by some microbes that inhibit the growth of other microbes.
Penicillin was the first antibiotic discovered by Alexander Fleming from Penicillium notatum.
Microbes in sewage treatment involve aerobic and anaerobic processes to reduce BOD.
Biogas is produced by methanogenic bacteria in anaerobic conditions.
Bacillus thuringiensis is used as a biocontrol agent against insect pests.
Rhizobium forms symbiotic association with leguminous plants and fixes nitrogen.
The universe expanded and hence, the temperature came down after Big Bang.
Hydrogen and Helium formed sometime later after Big Bang.
The gases condensed under gravitation and formed the galaxies of the present day universe.
options: Condensed under gravitation to form galaxies

question: There was no atmosphere on early earth. Water vapour, methane, carbondioxide and ammonia released from molten mass covered the surface.
The UV rays from the sun broke up water into Hydrogen and Oxygen and the lighter H₂ escaped.
Oxygen combined with ammonia and methane to form water, CO₂ and others. The ozone layer was formed.
As it cooled, the water vapor fell as rain, to fill all the depressions and form oceans.
options: Fell as rain to form oceans

question: In adults, each testis is oval in shape, with a length of about 4 to 5 cm and a width of about 2 to 3 cm.
The testis is covered by a dense covering. Each testis has about 250 compartments called testicular lobules.
Each seminiferous tubule is lined on its inside by two types of cells called male germ cells (spermatogonia) and Sertoli cells.
The regions outside the seminiferous tubules called interstitial spaces contain small blood vessels and interstitial cells or Leydig cells.
Leydig cells synthesise and secrete testicular hormones called androgens.
Curd is produced by Lactobacillus acidophilus.
Idli and dosa are fermented foods produced by bacteria and yeast.
Antibiotics like penicillin are produced by Penicillium notatum.
Microbes help in production of enzymes like lipases, proteases, etc.
In sewage treatment, aerobic microbes in aeration tank reduce BOD.
Panspermia is the theory that life came from outer space in the form of spores.
Louis Pasteur dismissed the theory of spontaneous generation by his experiment.
The first form of life could have come from pre-existing non-living organic molecules.
Miller's experiment showed formation of amino acids under simulated primitive earth conditions.
The male sex accessory glands include seminal vesicle, prostate and bulbourethral glands.
The seminal vesicles produce a fluid that is rich in fructose, calcium and some enzymes.
options: Rich in fructose, calcium and enzymes

question: The prostate gland produces a milky secretion that is slightly alkaline.
The bulbourethral glands secrete mucus that helps in lubrication of the penis.
The penis is the male external genitalia made up of special tissue that helps in erection.
Microbes in production of biogas involve methanogens in anaerobic sludge digesters.
Trichoderma is used as a biocontrol agent against plant pathogens.
Azospirillum is a free-living nitrogen fixing bacterium used as biofertiliser.
The first cellular forms of life appeared around 3 billion years ago.
The first organisms were probably chemoheterotrophs.
The origin of first eukaryotic cell is believed to have occurred around 2 billion years ago.
The female reproductive system includes a pair of ovaries, oviducts, uterus, cervix, vagina and external genitalia.
options: Ovaries, oviducts, uterus, cervix, vagina, external genitalia

question: The ovaries are the primary female sex organs that produce the female gamete (ovum) and several steroid hormones.
Each ovary is about 2 to 4 cm in length and is connected to the pelvic wall and uterus by ligaments.
The oviducts (fallopian tubes) are about 10-12 cm long and extend from the periphery of each ovary to the uterus.
The uterus is a hollow, pear-shaped organ that is highly muscular and capable of strong contractions during parturition.
Mycorrhiza is a symbiotic association between fungi and plant roots that helps in nutrient uptake.
Cyanobacteria like Nostoc and Anabaena are used as biofertilisers in rice fields.
Adaptive radiation is the process by which different species evolve from a common ancestor in different habitats.
Darwin's finches are an example of adaptive radiation.
The mechanism of evolution includes natural selection, genetic drift, mutation, gene flow, etc.
options: Natural selection, genetic drift, mutation, gene flow

question: Gametogenesis is the process of formation of gametes.
Spermatogenesis occurs in seminiferous tubules and produces sperms.
Oogenesis occurs in ovaries and produces ovum.
The menstrual cycle is a series of events in the female reproductive system that occurs approximately every 28 days.
Fertilisation occurs in the ampullary region of the oviduct.
Implantation occurs in the uterus where the blastocyst attaches to the endometrium.
Pregnancy lasts approximately 9 months or 280 days in humans.
Parturition is the process of delivery of the baby.
Lactation is the process of milk production by mammary glands.
Bt cotton is a genetically modified crop that produces toxin against bollworms.
Insulin is produced by recombinant DNA technology using E. coli.
Gene therapy is used to treat genetic disorders by replacing defective genes.
Transgenic animals are those that have foreign DNA incorporated into their genome.
Ethical issues in biotechnology include concerns about GM crops and cloning.
options: Concerns about GM crops and cloning

question: Productivity is the rate at which biomass is produced in an ecosystem.
Primary productivity is the rate at which solar energy is converted to chemical energy by producers.
options: Solar energy to chemical by producers

question: Decomposition is the process by which complex organic matter is broken down to inorganic substances.
Energy flow in an ecosystem is unidirectional from producers to consumers to decomposers.
Ecological pyramids represent the number, biomass or energy at each trophic level.
India is one of the 12 mega-diversity countries of the world.
Hotspots are regions with high species richness and endemism that are under threat.
options: High richness and endemism under threat

question: In-situ conservation includes national parks, sanctuaries and biosphere reserves.
Ex-situ conservation includes zoos, botanical gardens and cryopreservation.
The IUCN Red List is a catalogue of threatened species.
The genetic code is triplet, degenerate, universal and non-overlapping.
Transcription is the process of copying DNA to RNA.
Translation is the process of protein synthesis from mRNA.
The Human Genome Project was completed in 2003 and mapped the entire human genome.
DNA fingerprinting is a technique to identify individuals based on VNTRs.
Sewage treatment involves primary, secondary and tertiary processes.
The Hardy-Weinberg principle states that allele frequencies remain constant in a population in the absence of evolutionary forces.
A brief account of evolution includes origin of life, evolution of prokaryotes to eukaryotes, and origin of man.
options: Origin of life to origin of man

question: Origin and evolution of man includes Australopithecus, Homo habilis, Homo erectus, Neanderthal and Homo sapiens.
The menstrual cycle involves follicular phase, ovulation and luteal phase.
Fertilisation leads to formation of zygote which develops into blastocyst.
Pregnancy and embryonic development involve placenta formation for nutrient exchange.
Parturition is induced by oxytocin and involves strong uterine contractions.
Lactation is controlled by prolactin and oxytocin.
Transgenic animals are used in research for testing safety of vaccines.
Ethical issues include patenting of life forms and biopiracy.
Energy flow follows 10% law where only 10% energy is transferred to next trophic level.
Ecological pyramids can be upright or inverted depending on the ecosystem.
Nutrient cycling includes carbon cycle, nitrogen cycle, etc.
Loss of biodiversity is due to habitat loss, over-exploitation, alien species invasion and co-extinctions.
options: Habitat loss, over-exploitation, alien species, co-extinctions

question: Conservation efforts include both in-situ and ex-situ methods.
Regulation of gene expression includes lac operon in prokaryotes.
The Human Genome Project revealed about 20,000-25,000 genes in humans.
DNA fingerprinting is a technique to identify individuals based on VNTRs.
Microbes in household products include production of curd by Lactobacillus.
Microbes in industrial products include production of citric acid by Aspergillus niger.
